# Figure description: Схема_изоглубин_по_уровню_T4.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/plan_data/Схема_изоглубин_по_уровню_T4.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **isoline (isodepth) map** — specifically a **схема изоглубин** (depth contour scheme/map) constructed on a reference seismic reflector horizon designated **T₄**. It is a regional geophysical interpretation map compiled at a scale of **1:500,000**.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a large region in **northern Kazakhstan** (Казахская ССР), compiled by the **Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Association, Central Geological Prospecting Expedition** (Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение, Центральная геолого-поисковая экспедиция), under the **Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR** (Министерство геологии Каз.ССР).

- **Year**: 1964 г. (visible in upper portion) — though the report context places compilation in 1983–1984, this map may be a base/reference map from 1964 used within the later report
- The map layout (схема расположения листов) shown in the legend area references sheet codes including **XXXIII, XXXIV, LIV** and numbered sub-sheets (**I, II, III, VI, VII, VIII, IX, XII, XIV, XV**), consistent with 1:200,000 map sheet subdivisions of the M-42/N-42 series
- The note references **"К отчету Западно-Тенизской партии за 1918–64 гг."** (To the report of the West-Teniz Party for 1918–64)
- Geographic reference: **Тенизская впадина** (Teniz Depression/Basin) — the **boundary of the Teniz Plain** (Граница Тенизской впадины) is explicitly shown
- Source cited: **"Из отчета Котлярова А.И. за 1964 г., ЦКПГЭ"** (From the report of Kotlyarov A.I. for 1964, Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Expedition)
- The map carries a **state archive stamp** with inventory number **36572** (visible as a blue rectangular stamp near center)

---

## Key Content

### Isodepth Contours (Изоглубины):
- Contour lines depict the **depth to the T₄ seismic reflector**, which corresponds to a major subsurface stratigraphic/structural boundary (likely the top of the Paleozoic basement or a key sedimentary horizon)
- **Depth scale (Шкала интенсивности изоглубин, в м)**: contour intervals appear to range from approximately **600 m to beyond 1000+ m** (values of ~600, 800, 1000, 1200+ m are implied by the scale bar shown in the legend area)
- **Contour patterns reveal major structural elements**:
  - **Uplifts/domes** (structural highs): visible as closed oval/circular contour loops, shaded in **pink/salmon color**, indicating shallower depths to T₄ — several distinct dome-like closures are visible across the map, particularly in the western and central-western portions
  - **Depressions/basins** (structural lows): represented by darker shading (dark grey to black fill), indicating **greatest depths** to T₄ — prominent deep zones are visible in the northern and northeastern sectors
  - **Gradient zones**: closely spaced contours indicating steep structural flanks, suggestive of fault-controlled margins

### Structural Framework:
- The **Teniz Depression** (Тенизская впадина) occupies a significant portion of the map, outlined by a boundary line
- Multiple **anticlinal closures** (potential hydrocarbon or ore-bearing traps) are visible as pink-shaded highs, particularly in the west-central area
- A major **northeast-trending structural low** (deep basin) dominates the northern portion

### Fault and Disruption Zones (from legend):
- **Cross-hatched zones** (dark diagonal hatching): **Зоны потери корреляции 90, предположительно связанные с разрывными нарушениями или крутыми залеганиями слоев** — Zones of correlation loss >90%, presumably associated with faults/dislocations or steeply dipping beds
- **Diagonal hatching (lighter)**: **Разрывные нарушения предположительно по сейсморазведочным данным** — Inferred faults based on seismic data
- **Dark stippled/shaded patches**: **Выходы на поверхность отложений владимировской свиты в базе древних пород по сейсмическим данным** — Surface/near-surface outcrops of Vladimirka Suite deposits at the base of ancient rocks (seismic data)
- **Lighter stippled patches**: **Выходы на поверхность отложений владимировской свиты в зоне древних пород по геологическим данным** — Same unit outcrops by geological data
- **Dashed boundary line**: **Граница Тенизской впадины** — Boundary of the Teniz Depression

### Color Scheme:
- **Pink/salmon**: structural highs (shallow T₄)
- **White/uncolored**: intermediate depths
- **Dark grey/black fill**: deepest zones (structural lows, possible salt structures or deep basins)
- **Cross-hatching**: seismically disrupted zones (faults, steep dips, correlation loss)
